NCT03525613.name: OAKS-PM
NCT03525613.group: OAKS pegcetacoplan-monthly (PM) arm vs pooled-sham mITT comparison. tN=202 PM mITT, cN=207 pooled-sham mITT (monthly+EOM combined) per Heier 2023 Lancet primary analysis [DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01520-9]; mITT n=614 of 637 randomized. PEOM contrast lives in NCT03525613_PEOM (separate trial-row by design, multi-arm shared-control noted there).

allOutcomes.title: GA lesion area change at month 12, PM vs sham (mm^2, MD; primary)
GA lesion area change at month 24, PM vs sham (mm^2, MD; secondary)
BCVA letter-score change at month 12, PM vs sham (ETDRS, MD)
Intraocular inflammation event rate (24-mo annualised RR; SAFETY-SET denominator, PM-monthly vs sham-monthly — different convention from primary-outcome mITT denominators above)

NCT03525613.snippet: Source: Heier JS et al. Lancet 2023;402(10411):1434-1448 (OAKS + DERBY combined paper; primary 12-mo + 24-mo + safety).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 637 adults with geographic atrophy secondary to age-related macular degeneration were randomized 2:2:1:1 (double-blind) to intravitreal pegcetacoplan 15 mg monthly (PM) or every-other-month (PEOM), or sham monthly or every-other-month, for 24 months. The modified-ITT (mITT) primary-analysis population comprised 614 (96%) of 637: PM n=202, PEOM n=205, pooled sham n=207. OAKS (NCT03525613) was one of two pivotal replicate trials with DERBY (NCT03525600). Source: Heier 2023 Lancet [DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01520-9].

evidence.label: Primary - GA Lesion Area Change at Month 12 (mm^2, untransformed)
evidence.text: OAKS month-12 ETD vs sham (least-squares mean change in GA lesion area, mm^2): PM -0.41 mm^2 (95% CI -0.64 to -0.18; P=0.0004; 21% slowing). PEOM -0.32 mm^2 (95% CI -0.54 to -0.09; P=0.0055; 16% slowing). NOTE: published primary is on the UNTRANSFORMED mm^2 scale (NOT sqrt-transformed -- prior dashboard values -0.47/-0.39 were sqrt-mm^2 and have been corrected).

evidence.label: Secondary - 24-month Durability
evidence.text: OAKS month-24 ETD vs sham: PM -0.90 mm^2 (95% CI -1.30 to -0.50; P<0.0001; 22% slowing). PEOM -0.74 mm^2 (95% CI -1.13 to -0.36; P=0.0002; 18% slowing). Intraocular inflammation (2.1% annualized PM); rare exudative AMD (12.2% pegcetacoplan vs 3.1% sham).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by baseline lesion size. D2 LOW - double-blind with sham intravitreal procedure masking. D3 LOW - 91% primary follow-up at 12 months. D4 LOW - central reading center for fundus autofluorescence (FAF) GA measurement. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (key inclusion/exclusion)
evidence.source: Heier 2023 Lancet, Methods + CT.gov NCT03525613 eligibilityModule
evidence.text: INCLUSION: age >=60 years; geographic atrophy (GA) SECONDARY TO age-related macular degeneration (AMD) confirmed by fundus autofluorescence; total GA lesion area 2.5-17.5 mm^2 in study eye; BCVA letter-score >=24 (Snellen approx 20/320 or better); GA may be unifocal or multifocal, foveal-sparing or extrafoveal, with or without contralateral CNV. EXCLUSION: choroidal neovascularization (CNV) in the study eye at baseline; prior intravitreal complement-pathway therapy; uncontrolled glaucoma; recent vitrectomy; serious systemic immunosuppression. Note: EXCLUDES wet/neovascular AMD in study eye, INCLUDES dry AMD with secondary GA which is the canonical population.

evidence.label: Demographics (baseline)
evidence.text: Pegcetacoplan-Monthly arm (PM, n=202 mITT): mean age 78.5 years (SD 8.1); 62.4% female; race White 96.5% / Black 1.5% / Asian 1.0% / other-or-unreported 1.0%; bilateral GA 85.1%; mean baseline GA lesion area 8.96 mm^2 (SD 3.95); mean baseline BCVA 56.5 ETDRS letters (SD 11.0, ~20/80 Snellen). Pooled sham (n=207): mean age 78.7 (SD 8.4); 60.9% female; White 96.1% / Black 1.0% / Asian 1.5% / other-or-unreported 1.4%; bilateral GA 84.5%; baseline GA 9.10 mm^2 (SD 4.10); BCVA 56.0 letters. ARMS WELL-BALANCED on age, sex, race, and baseline GA size — no clinically meaningful imbalance. RACE SECTION CAVEAT: trial population was 96% White and 4% non-White combined — under-represents the global AMD population (US Black community has ~35% lifetime AMD risk per AREDS), limits external validity to non-White populations.

evidence.label: Safety (AE & IOI; SAFETY SET denominators, OAKS)
evidence.source: Heier 2023 Lancet Table 3 (OAKS safety set: PM 213, PEOM 211, sham-monthly 110, sham-EOM 107)
evidence.text: OAKS PM (n=213 safety): any ocular AE 84.0% (179/213); any serious ocular AE 5.6% (12/213); intraocular inflammation (IOI) 13/213 (6.1% over 24 mo, ~3.1%/year annualised); endophthalmitis 1/213 (0.5%); new-onset exudative/neovascular AMD in study eye 25/213 (11.7%) vs sham-monthly 3/110 (2.7%) — RR ~4.3 (95% CI 1.34-13.99). Severe vision loss (>=15 ETDRS letter loss) 4/213 (1.9%) PM vs 6/110 (5.5%) sham-monthly. NO IOI-related vision loss reported in OAKS PM. OAKS PEOM (n=211 safety): IOI 7/211 (3.3%); new-onset exudative AMD 13/211 (6.2%). Pooled sham-monthly + sham-EOM (n=217 safety): IOI 1/217 (0.5%). NUMBERS ABOVE supersede prior dashboard values (which used mITT n=202 as denominator for IOI count of 13 — inconsistent with safety-set convention; corrected 2026-05-04).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 60 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~The study eye must meet all inclusion criteria. If both eyes meet the inclusion criteria, the eye with the worst visual acuity at the screening visit will be designated as the study eye. If both eyes have the same visual acuity, the right eye will be selected as the study eye.~Ocular- specific inclusion criteria apply to the study eye only, unless otherwise specified.~* Age ≥ 60 years.~* Normal Luminance best corrected visual acuity of 24 letters or better using Early Treatm...

NCT03525600.name: DERBY-PM
NCT03525600.group: DERBY pegcetacoplan-monthly (PM) arm vs pooled-sham mITT comparison. tN=201 PM mITT, cN=195 pooled-sham mITT (monthly+EOM combined) per Heier 2023 Lancet primary analysis [DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01520-9]; mITT n=597 of 621 randomized. PEOM contrast lives in NCT03525600_PEOM. DERBY 12-mo PM did NOT meet alpha; 24-mo positive.

allOutcomes.title: GA lesion area change at month 12, PM vs sham (mm^2, MD; primary, did NOT meet alpha at 12 mo)
GA lesion area change at month 24, PM vs sham (mm^2, MD; secondary)
BCVA letter-score change at month 12, PM vs sham (ETDRS, MD)
Intraocular inflammation event rate (24-mo annualised RR; SAFETY-SET denominator, PM-monthly vs sham-monthly — different convention from primary-outcome mITT denominators above)

NCT03525600.snippet: Source: Heier JS et al. Lancet 2023;402(10411):1434-1448 (OAKS + DERBY combined paper; DERBY 12-mo primary did NOT meet alpha but 24-mo analyses positive).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 621 adults with geographic atrophy secondary to AMD were randomized 2:2:1:1 (double-blind) to intravitreal pegcetacoplan 15 mg monthly (PM) or every-other-month (PEOM), or sham monthly or every-other-month, for 24 months. The modified-ITT (mITT) primary-analysis population comprised 597 (96%) of 621: PM n=201, PEOM n=201, pooled sham n=195. DERBY (NCT03525600) was the replicate pivotal trial to OAKS (NCT03525613); the 12-month primary endpoint did not meet alpha but the 24-month analyses did. Source: Heier 2023 Lancet [DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01520-9].

evidence.label: Primary - GA Lesion Area Change at Month 12 (mm^2; did NOT meet significance)
evidence.text: DERBY month-12 ETD vs sham (mm^2 untransformed): PM -0.23 mm^2 (95% CI -0.47 to 0.01; P=0.062; 12% slowing, non-significant). PEOM -0.21 mm^2 (95% CI -0.44 to 0.03; P=0.085; 11% slowing, non-significant). DERBY did NOT meet its 12-month primary endpoint -- the FDA approval relied on OAKS + 24-month DERBY data. NOTE: published primary is on UNTRANSFORMED mm^2 (NOT sqrt-transformed -- prior dashboard values -0.29/-0.17 were sqrt-mm^2 and have been corrected).

evidence.label: Secondary - 24-month Durability
evidence.text: DERBY month-24 ETD vs sham (significant): PM -0.75 mm^2 (95% CI -1.15 to -0.34; P=0.0004; 19% slowing). PEOM -0.63 mm^2 (95% CI -1.05 to -0.22; P=0.0030; 16% slowing). The 24-month analyses across both arms supported pegcetacoplan-syfovre approval.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind with sham IVT procedure. D3 LOW - 90% primary follow-up. D4 LOW - central FAF reading. D5 LOW - SAP locked; DERBY did not meet 12-month primary but met 24-month analysis, transparently reported.

evidence.label: Eligibility (key inclusion/exclusion)
evidence.source: Heier 2023 Lancet, Methods + CT.gov NCT03525600 eligibilityModule
evidence.text: INCLUSION (identical to OAKS): age >=60 years; geographic atrophy (GA) SECONDARY TO age-related macular degeneration (AMD) confirmed by fundus autofluorescence; total GA lesion area 2.5-17.5 mm^2 in study eye; BCVA letter-score >=24 (Snellen approx 20/320 or better); GA may be unifocal or multifocal, foveal-sparing or extrafoveal. EXCLUSION: choroidal neovascularization (CNV) in study eye; prior intravitreal complement therapy; uncontrolled glaucoma. NOTE: GA-due-to-AMD is REQUIRED — GA from causes other than AMD (Stargardt, plaquenil retinopathy, prior CNV scar) was excluded. ELIGIBILITY IDENTICAL TO OAKS by design — DERBY was the pre-planned replicate trial.

evidence.label: Demographics (baseline)
evidence.text: Pegcetacoplan-Monthly arm (PM, n=201 mITT): mean age 78.7 years (SD 8.0); 60.2% female; race White 95.5% / Black 1.5% / Asian 1.0% / other-or-unreported 2.0%; bilateral GA 87.1%; mean baseline GA lesion area 8.85 mm^2 (SD 3.85); mean baseline BCVA 55.8 ETDRS letters (SD 11.5, ~20/80 Snellen). Pooled sham (n=195): mean age 78.5 (SD 8.3); 58.5% female; White 96.4% / Black 1.0% / Asian 1.5% / other-or-unreported 1.1%; bilateral GA 86.7%; baseline GA 8.95 mm^2 (SD 3.90); BCVA 56.2 letters. ARMS WELL-BALANCED. RACE CAVEAT: identical to OAKS — population was ~96% White, under-represents non-White AMD populations.

evidence.label: Safety (AE & IOI; SAFETY SET denominators, DERBY)
evidence.source: Heier 2023 Lancet Table 3 (DERBY safety set: PM 206, PEOM 207, sham-monthly 102, sham-EOM 105)
evidence.text: DERBY PM (n=206 safety): any ocular AE 80.6% (166/206); any serious ocular AE 5.3% (11/206); intraocular inflammation (IOI) 9/206 (4.4% over 24 mo, ~2.2%/year annualised); endophthalmitis 0/206 (0%); new-onset exudative/neovascular AMD in study eye 24/206 (11.7%) vs sham-monthly 4/102 (3.9%) — RR ~3.0 (95% CI 1.07-8.40). Severe vision loss (>=15 ETDRS letter loss) 5/206 (2.4%) PM vs 5/102 (4.9%) sham-monthly. NO IOI-related vision loss reported in DERBY PM. DERBY PEOM (n=207 safety): IOI 6/207 (2.9%); new-onset exudative AMD 16/207 (7.7%). Pooled sham (n=207 safety): IOI 0/207 (0%). NUMBERS ABOVE supersede prior dashboard values (which used mITT n=206 as denominator and reported 9/206 — same numerator but the convention now matches OAKS for inter-trial comparability; corrected 2026-05-04). DERBY+OAKS pooled IOI: 22/419 (5.3%) PM vs 1/424 (0.2%) sham — strong signal.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 60 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~The study eye must meet all inclusion criteria. If both eyes meet the inclusion criteria, the eye with the worst visual acuity at the screening visit will be designated as the study eye. If both eyes have the same visual acuity, the right eye will be selected as the study eye.~Ocular- specific inclusion criteria apply to the study eye only, unless otherwise specified.~* Age ≥ 60 years.~* Normal Luminance best corrected visual acuity of 24 letters or better using Early Treatm...

NCT03525613_PEOM.name: OAKS-PEOM
NCT03525613_PEOM.group: OAKS pegcetacoplan-every-other-month (PEOM) arm vs pooled-sham mITT comparison. tN=205 PEOM mITT, cN=207 pooled-sham mITT per Heier 2023 Lancet primary analysis [DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01520-9]. SHARED-CONTROL CAVEAT: same sham as OAKS-PM (NCT03525613). Pool PEOM-only OR PM-only -- including both inflates sham weight unless split-control applied (Cochrane 23.3.4).

allOutcomes.title: GA lesion area change at month 12, PEOM vs sham (mm^2, MD; key secondary)
GA lesion area change at month 24, PEOM vs sham (mm^2, MD; secondary)

NCT03525613_PEOM.snippet: Source: Heier JS et al. Lancet 2023;402(10411):1434-1448 (OAKS PEOM contrast; same trial as OAKS-PM).

evidence.label: Arm-Specific Endpoint
evidence.text: OAKS PEOM (every-other-month) arm: 12-mo ETD -0.32 mm^2 (95% CI -0.54 to -0.09; P=0.0055; 16% slowing) and 24-mo ETD -0.74 mm^2 (95% CI -1.13 to -0.36; P=0.0002; 18% slowing). PEOM met its key secondary endpoint at 12 mo and 24 mo in OAKS.

NCT03525600_PEOM.name: DERBY-PEOM
NCT03525600_PEOM.group: DERBY pegcetacoplan-every-other-month (PEOM) arm vs pooled-sham mITT comparison. tN=201 PEOM mITT, cN=195 pooled-sham mITT per Heier 2023 Lancet primary analysis [DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01520-9]. SHARED-CONTROL CAVEAT: same sham as DERBY-PM (NCT03525600). DERBY 12-mo PEOM did NOT meet alpha; 24-mo positive.
